title: Attributes
description: Key-value pairs on the opening tag.

questions.prompt: Which is a correctly written attribute?
questions.explanation: attribute="value" with an equals sign and quoted value is the canonical form.

questions.prompt: Which attribute is BOOLEAN — its presence alone is enough?
questions.explanation: Boolean attributes like disabled, checked, required don't need a value.

questions.prompt: Give this input a name and mark it required.
questions.explanation: Forms submit inputs by their name. required makes the field mandatory.
class and CLASS behave the same. Convention: lowercase.

questions.prompt: Which is an example of a GLOBAL attribute (works on any element)?
questions.explanation: id, class, style, hidden, title, tabindex, data-* are globals.

questions.prompt: Custom data attributes use the prefix:
questions.explanation: data-* attributes are official. Read from JS as element.dataset.camelCase.

questions.prompt: How does JS read `data-user-id` from an element?
Boolean attribute short forms (like `disabled` alone) mean:
questions.explanation: The presence of a boolean attribute means true. Value doesn't matter — even disabled="false" is truthy.
